design_philosophy.core_vibe: Vegas Nightclub meets Pro DJ Software
key_principles: Dark Mode Only: The app lives in bars and venues. No light mode.
High Contrast: Text must pop against dark backgrounds.
Tactical Controls: Host buttons must be large and mistake-proof.
Audience Spectacle: The TV view is for entertainment, not just data.

usage_rules.headings: Use 'Anton' for all major headers, round titles, and the 'BINGO' shout. Tracking-wide for effect.
usage_rules.body: Use 'Manrope' for all UI controls, lists, and settings. It has excellent legibility for numbers.
usage_rules.numbers: Use 'Manrope' (Bold) or 'Anton' for the Bingo Numbers. They are the stars of the show.

semantic_usage.bingo_button: Gold (#EAB308) or Bright Green (#22C55E) for the 'Verify Bingo' moment.

structure: Header: Timer, Round Info, Audience Toggle
Main Right (1/3): Current Number, Last 5 Numbers, Control Panel
Bottom: Bingo Board Grid (Small view for verification)
host_dashboard.spacing: Compact (p-4) to fit everything on a laptop screen.

structure: Bottom: Recently Called Numbers (Horizontal Scroll or Grid)

custom_components.name: BingoBall
custom_components.description: A 3D-looking sphere using CSS radial gradients. Animates in when called.

custom_components.name: VideoPlayerFrame
custom_components.description: Wrapper for the video player with a neon border and 'Now Playing' overlay.

custom_components.name: CelebrationOverlay
custom_components.description: Full-screen overlay with confetti and 'BINGO!' text animation.

shadcn_customizations.Button: Rounded-md, font-bold. Primary buttons should have a subtle 'glow' shadow.

image_urls.description: Abstract Neon Green/Blue/Red - Good for Audience Background
Disco Ball Dark - Good for Lobby or Wait Screen

animations: BallRoll: CSS keyframe animation for the ball entering the screen.
NumberPop: Scale up/down bounce effect when a number is called.
Confetti: Use 'canvas-confetti' library for the win state.
motion.transitions: All interactive elements must have `transition-all duration-200 ease-out`.

universal_guidelines: Dark colors look good when used independently without gradients. DO NOT use dark colors as gradients
Don't make generic centered layouts, simplistic gradients, and uniform styling.
Create depth through layered design elements with z-index hierarchy
Use glass-morphism effects with backdrop filters (12-24px blur)
You MUST NOT apply universal transition. Eg: `transition: all`. This results in breaking transforms. Always add transitions for specific interactive elements like button, input excluding transforms
You MUST NOT center align the app container, ie do not add `.App { text-align: center; }` in the css file. This disrupts the human natural reading flow of text
Always use modern button styles like pill-shaped, or sharp button with interaction animations & color relevant to the app style
NEVER: use AI assistant Emoji characters like 🤖🧠💭💡🔮 etc for icons.
Every interaction needs micro-animations - hover states, transitions, parallax effects, and entrance animations. Static = dead.
Use 2-3x more spacing than feels comfortable. Cramped designs look cheap.
Subtle grain textures, noise overlays, custom cursors, selection states, and loading animations: separates good from extraordinary.
Main agent relies on these image URLs. Missing categories (especially sponsor logos) = main agent will use placeholder text which is FORBIDDEN
Prioritize using pre-existing components from src/components/ui when applicable
Components MUST use named exports (export const ComponentName = ...)
Pages MUST use default exports (export default function PageName() {...})
instructions_to_main_agent: You are building the 'Trivia-Maestro_V4' sibling app. It must feel like part of the same suite. Use the provided colors and fonts strictly. The Host Dashboard is your complex view; keep it organized. The Audience View is your 'Art' view; make it beautiful and readable from 20 feet away.
